title: A wrist support arrangement and other components
abstract: A movable wrist support for use with a computer peripheral is described. The movable wrist support is used in conjunction with a mouse, but it may be used with a keyboard, or other peripheral. Also described is a support bed for supporting a movable wrist support thereon. The support bed and/or the movable wrist support may be integral with or attach-able to a keyboard or other peripheral. The support bed may include some guidance for, and/or limits on, the movable wrist support. Also described is a keyboard which includes a plurality of orthogonal rows of keys, juxtaposed with one or more staggered rows of keys. The arrangement may be such that there are left and right keypads, wherein the orthogo-nal and staggered rows on the left and right keypads are arranged on the keyboard at an angle to a longitudinal line extending along the keyboard, away from the user.
claims: Claims Claims 1. A movable wrist support for use with a computer peripheral, the movable wrist support configured to readily move across a surface adjacent the computer peripheral so as to support the wrist and move with the wrist during wrist movement on the surface. 2. The movable wrist support in accordance with claim 1 , the wrist support being a car riage or skate for carrying a wrist around on a surface. 3. The movable wrist support in accordance with claim 1 or 2 wherein the carriage or skate includes a chassis for structure and a support pad for wrist comfort. 4. The movable wrist support in accordance with claim 3 wherein the chassis is in the form of a mounting base plate. 5. The movable wrist support in accordance with any one of claims 1 to 4 wherein the carriage or skate includes friction- reducing elements mounted on the mounting base. 6. The movable wrist support in accordance with claim 5 wherein the friction-reducing elements are in the form of low-friction slider tabs. 7. The movable wrist support in accordance with claim 6 wherein the low friction tabs are constructed from or wholly constructed from PTFE, or coated with or wholly con structed from PTFE. 8. The movable wrist support in accordance with any one of claims 1 to 7 including wheels or castors or balls on which to roll. 9. The movable wrist support in accordance with any one of claims 3 to 8 wherein the support pad is constructed from resilient material or assembly including a sprung board, open cell foam, memory foam, gel, or other suitable material for comfortable resting. 10. The movable wrist support in accordance with any one of claims 6 to 9 wherein the or each slider tab is in the form of an elongate element so as to resist peel along a longi tudinal axis. 11. The movable wrist support in accordance with any one of claims 1 to 10 wherein at least a portion of the or each slider tab is extensible from the base plate or otherwise configured to be disposed in a position slightly distal the base face of the base plate, in order to provide variability in the height or attitude of the carriage or skate. 12. The movable wrist support in accordance with any one of claims 6 to 11 wherein at least a portion of the slider tab is mounted on more adjustable stands so as to adjust the angle of the base plate relative to a desk on which the carriage is disposed. 13. The movable wrist support in accordance with claim 12 wherein the one or more ad justable stands are disposed in one or more corners of the base plate. 14. The movable wrist support in accordance with claim 12 or 13 wherein the one or more stands are configured to adopt a stowed position so as to be substantially flush with the base plate face and a deployed position wherein the one or more stands are ex tended from the face of the base plate. 15. The movable wrist support in accordance with any one of claims 1 to 14 further in cluding one or more fasteners for fastening to an input/ouput peripheral such as a mouse, keyboard, joystick or other input device. 16. The movable wrist support in accordance with claim 15 wherein the fastener includes a magnet, bracket, boss, clip or buckle. 17. The movable wrist support in accordance with any one of claims 1 to 16 including a wrist strap for mounting to a wrist. 18. The movable wrist support in accordance with any one of claims 3 to 17 including a contoured wrist support element disposed on the plate. 19. The movable wrist support in accordance with any one of claims 5 to 18 wherein there are a plurality of low friction elements disposed so as to be adjacent the side edges of the base plate and to extend parallel to those side edges. 20. A support bed suitable for supporting the movable wrist support of any one of claims 1 to 19 thereon, the support bed including a base for sitting on a desk, and a top sur face defining a wrist support zone for movably supporting the movable wrist support thereon. 21. The support bed in accordance with claim 20, including a top surface being a portion of a panel of glass. 22. The support bed in accordance with claim 20 or 21 including one or more peripheral walls in one or more side regions of the top surface so as to keep the movable wrist support in the wrist support zone. 23. The support bed in accordance with any one of claims 20 to 22 wherein the peripheral walls are about 1 to 10 millimetres high to provide a stop for the movable wrist sup port. 24. The support bed in accordance with any one of claims 20 to 23 including one or more adjustable stands so as to adjust the height or attitude of the base plate relative to a desk on which the carriage is disposed. 25. The support bed in accordance with any one of claims 20 to 24 wherein the one or more adjustable stands are disposed in one or more corners of the base. 26. The support bed in accordance with any one of claims 20 to 25 wherein the one or more stands are configured to adopt a stowed position so as to be substantially flush with the base and a deployed position wherein the one or more stands are extended from the base. 27. The support bed in accordance with any one of claims 20 to 26 further including a fric tion reduction layer disposed at the top layer. 28. The support bed in accordance with claim 27 wherein the friction reduction layer is glass. 29. The support bed in accordance with any one of claims 20 to 28 wherein the glass is tempered. 30. The support bed in accordance with any one of claims 20 to 29 further including a removable wrist support pad which is substantially the same size as the wrist support zone. 31. The support bed in accordance with any one of claims 20 to 30 further including one or more fasteners for fastening to an input/ouput peripheral such as a mouse, key board, joystick or other input device. 32. The support bed in accordance with claim 31 wherein the fastener includes a magnet, bracket, boss, clip or buckle. 33. A wrist support kit including a support bed in accordance with any one of claims 20 to 32, and a movable wrist support in accordance with any one of claims 1 to 19 for movement on the support bed. 34. The wrist support kit in accordance with claim 33 further including a removable wrist support pad in accordance with claim 30. 35. A keyboard with an integral support bed, or attached support bed, which includes a wrist support zone for supporting a movable wrist support thereon. 36. The keyboard in accordance with claim 35 further including at least one base which includes a key support zone disposed in a region of the base which is relatively distal the user, and a wrist support zone, in a region of the base which is disposed relatively proximal the user. 37. The keyboard in accordance with claim 35 or 36 wherein the keyboard includes two bases, wherein a left base includes a left keypad, and the other base, the right base, includes a right keypad. 38. The keyboard in accordance with any one of claims 35 to 37 wherein the left and right keypads are substantially symmetrical in overall size and shape. 39. The keyboard in accordance with any one of claims 37 to 38 wherein the left base includes a wrist support bed integral therewith. 40. The keyboard in accordance with any one of claims 37 to 39 wherein the right base includes a wrist support bed integral therewith. 41. The keyboard in accordance with any one of claims 35 to 40 wherein the left and right bases are connectable to one another. 42. The keyboard in accordance with claim 41 wherein the left and right bases are elec trically connectable to one another, by way of a connector cable. 43. The keyboard in accordance with claim 41 or 42 wherein the left and right bases are removably connectable to one another by way of a clip, or magnets, hooks and loops, or other suitable fastening device. 44. The keyboard in accordance with any one of claims 35 to 43 wherein the base is larger in area in a region of the base that is proximal the user than a region of the base that is distal the user. 45. The keyboard in accordance with claim 45 wherein the base region proximal the user is wider than the distal region of the base, the arrangement being to facilitate user access to distal regions of the keyboard base. 46. The keyboard in accordance with any one of claims 35 to 45 wherein the bases are substantially trapezoidal. 47. The keyboard in accordance with any one of claim 46 wherein the arrangement of the bases is such that the rows of keys are disposed so that they extend generally normal to the angled legs of the right trapezoid. 48. The keyboard in accordance with any one of claims 35 to 47 wherein the support bed includes peripheral walls so as to retain the movable wrist support on the support bed. 49. The keyboard in accordance with claim 48 wherein the peripheral walls are a few mil limetres high to provide a stop for the movable wrist support. 50. The keyboard in accordance with any one of claims 35 to 49 wherein the support bed is overall generally in the same shape as the key support zone. 51. The keyboard in accordance with any one of claims 35 to 50 wherein the support bed is in the shape of a trapezoid to substantially match the shape of the key support zone to facilitate reach of all the keys while hands rest on the movable wrist support. 52. The keyboard in accordance with any one of claims 35 to 51 further including the movable wrist support in accordance with any one of claims 1 to 19. 53. The keyboard in accordance with any one of claims 35 to 52 further including the support bed integral with or fastened thereto in accordance with any one of claims 20 to 31. 54. A keyboard including a plurality of orthogonal rows of keys, juxtaposed with one or more staggered rows of keys. 55. The keyboard in accordance with claim 54 wherein there are provided left and right keypads, wherein the orthogonal and staggered rows on the left and right keypads are arranged on the keyboard at an angle to a longitudinal line extending along the keyboard, away from the user. 56. The keyboard in accordance with claim 54 or 55 wherein a key output associated with one or more keys may be reassignable by combined use with another key. 57. The keyboard in accordance with any one of claims 54 to 56 wherein the orthogonal keys are disposed in four orthogonal rows. 58. The keyboard in accordance with any one of claims 54 to 56 wherein in the orthogo nal rows, the keys are substantially aligned with one another in a matrix of rows and columns, such that there is provided a top row, which is disposed distal the user, and there is a base row, which is disposed on the base relatively proximal the user. 59. The keyboard in accordance with claim 57 or 58 wherein a further row, being Row 5, is disposed on a proximal side of the base orthogonal row, and includes keys that are laterally offset from the keys in the orthogonal rows, so as to facilitate ease of reach. 60. The keyboard in accordance with claim 59 wherein further row is provided, which has further lateral offsets relative to those keys on Row 5, the keys being larger in area to provide ease of use for frequent use such as in function keys or other keys which change key mapping. 61. The keyboard in accordance with any one of claims 54 to 60 wherein the left base and the right base of the keyboard are separable so as to provide a suitable selected angular disposition to suit the user. 62. The keyboard in accordance with any one of claims 54 to 6 wherein the keyboard in cludes a processor so as to facilitate a change in key mapping. 63. A keyboard including: a plurality of orthogonal rows of keys substantially aligned in rows and col umns, the orthogonal rows including a base row and a top row; and one or more staggered rows of keys, wherein the one or more staggered rows of keys includes a plurality of keys that are laterally offset from the keys in the orthogonal rows, the one or more stag gered rows being disposed adjacent the base or top orthogonal row of keys.
